book_claims.claim: Recommended unsweetened plant milk for the Longevity Diet, used at breakfast and as a snack beverage. Recommended serving: 1 glass (240 mL), unsweetened.
book_claims.mechanism: Provides a plant-based dairy alternative that avoids the IGF-1-stimulating effects associated with cow's milk protein while contributing healthy monounsaturated fats from hazelnuts.

supplementary_research.finding: Hazelnut consumption is associated with reduced LDL cholesterol and improved endothelial function due to its high monounsaturated fat and vitamin E content; these benefits extend to hazelnut-derived products. (PMID 28760423)

Plant-based milk alternatives, when unsweetened and fortified, provide comparable micronutrient delivery to cow's milk with lower saturated fat and no animal protein-driven IGF-1 elevation. (PMID 30274179)
Unsweetened hazelnut milk is low in calories and provides vitamin E and monounsaturated fats; fortified versions can supply calcium and vitamin D comparable to cow's milk.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Fat-soluble antioxidant; absorbed alongside the natural fat in hazelnut milk
Cardioprotective oleic acid from hazelnuts; readily absorbed
Bioavailability of fortified calcium carbonate is comparable to dairy calcium
Low caloric density supports caloric restriction patterns in the Longevity Diet

synergies.description: Classic Longevity Diet breakfast pairing; the fat in hazelnut milk aids absorption of fat-soluble vitamins in oats
Fat in hazelnut milk improves absorption of fat-soluble vitamin D when taken together at breakfast
Fortified hazelnut milk adds calcium and vitamin D to fortified breakfast cereals, creating a complementary micronutrient-dense breakfast
